question: How does the average car price vary across different manufacturers?

analysis.design_rationale: A boxplot allows exploration of price variability within each manufacturer along with their medians and outlier behavior. This approach goes beyond the mean by showing full price distributions, which is critical given the possibility of wide ranges (some manufacturers may have both entry-level and luxury models). Displaying top N manufacturers with the most listings prevents clutter, ensuring the boxplots remain interpretable and visually compelling.
analysis.target_insight: Price dispersion, medians, and outliers across the most popular manufacturers, revealing how price varies not just on average but in spread and extremes.

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Show outliers; rotate x-labels; highlight medians

transformations: Count number of listings per manufacturer
Select top 10 manufacturers by listing count
Filter original dataframe to these manufacturers
data_preparation_steps: boxplot_data = [df_top[df_top['Manufacturer'] == manu]['Price'] for manu in top10_manufacturers]
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\28.csv")
manufacturer_counts = df["Manufacturer"].value_counts()
top10_manufacturers = manufacturer_counts.head(10).index.tolist()
df_top = df[df["Manufacturer"].isin(top10_manufacturers)]
boxplot_data = [df_top[df_top["Manufacturer"] == manu]["Price"] for manu in top10_manufacturers]
plt.figure(figsize=(14, 8))
plt.boxplot(boxplot_data, labels=top10_manufacturers, vert=True, patch_artist=True, boxprops=dict(facecolor="lightgreen", alpha=0.5), medianprops=dict(color="darkred", linewidth=2))
plt.xlabel("Manufacturer (Top 10 by Listings)")
plt.ylabel("Car Price")
plt.title("Car Price Distribution by Top Manufacturers\n(Boxplot: Median, Spread, Outliers)")
plt.xticks(rotation=30, ha="right")
plt.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.4)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
